name: San Antonio
tagline: River Walk Charm, Tex-Mex Heaven, and No State Income Tax
description: San Antonio is one of America's most underrated FIRE destinations, offering big-city amenities with a remarkably low cost of living and zero state income tax. The iconic River Walk, a deeply rooted Tex-Mex food culture, and centuries of Spanish colonial history give the city a character unlike anywhere else in the country. With affordable housing, warm weather, and a genuine sense of community, San Antonio rewards budget-conscious retirees with a rich and flavorful daily life.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens
quickFacts.safetyRating: Moderate – typical large US city with safe suburban areas
quickFacts.climate: Subtropical with hot summers and mild winters

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Alamo Heights fringe, Southtown, or the Medical Center area

dining.description: Breakfast tacos and Tex-Mex joints 2-3x/week; cooking at home for most meals

healthcare.description: ACA marketplace Silver plan; Texas has no state exchange, costs can be higher

transportation.description: Paid-off car with basic insurance and gas; car is necessary in San Antonio

entertainment.description: Free River Walk strolls, the Alamo and missions, park trails, occasional Spurs game on a deal

1M.lifestyle: A modest apartment near the Medical Center or in Southtown, cooking at home most nights with affordable Tex-Mex ingredients, and a paid-off car to get around. The River Walk and the San Antonio Missions are free, and breakfast tacos from local taquerias cost a few dollars. Healthcare takes a meaningful share of the budget, and there is not much room for surprises, but zero state income tax helps every dollar go further.

housing.description: 2BR apartment in the Pearl District area, or a nice home in Alamo Heights or Terrell Hills

dining.description: Regular dining at Pearl District restaurants and River Walk spots 3-4x/week; weekend barbecue pilgrimages

healthcare.description: ACA Gold plan with dental and vision add-ons; access to excellent military medical facilities if eligible

transportation.description: Reliable mid-range car, full coverage insurance, gas and maintenance

entertainment.description: Spurs season tickets (upper level), San Antonio Museum of Art membership, live music at Gruene Hall

2M.lifestyle: A comfortable home in Alamo Heights or a nice apartment near the Pearl District, where you can dine out several times a week at the area's restaurants and food stalls. A reliable car covers errands and weekend drives to the Hill Country. You can afford Spurs tickets, a good health plan, and a few trips per year. San Antonio's moderate costs mean this budget provides a genuinely comfortable daily life.

housing.description: 3BR home in Olmos Park, Monte Vista, or a modern condo overlooking the River Walk

dining.description: Fine dining at Mixtli, Cured, and The Granary; regular River Walk dining; catered Tex-Mex feasts

healthcare.description: Premium private health insurance with comprehensive coverage and low deductibles

transportation.description: Late-model SUV, premium insurance, weekend road trips to Fredericksburg and the Hill Country

entertainment.description: Premium Spurs tickets, golf club access, San Antonio Symphony season tickets, spa treatments

3M.lifestyle: A spacious home in Olmos Park or Monte Vista, regular dinners at restaurants like Mixtli and Cured, and premium Spurs tickets. You can afford good private health insurance, a late-model SUV, and regular weekend trips to Fredericksburg and the Hill Country wine region. At this budget, San Antonio feels affluent -- you eat well, live in a desirable neighborhood, and have meaningful flexibility for travel and hobbies.

housing.description: Luxury estate in Terrell Hills, a renovated King William mansion, or upscale condo at The Broadway

dining.description: Private chef for dinner parties, fine dining multiple nights per week, exclusive wine pairing events

healthcare.description: Concierge medicine, executive health screenings, premium private insurance with no deductibles

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle, premium insurance, detailing service, Hill Country ranch vehicle

entertainment.description: Courtside Spurs tickets, San Antonio Country Club membership, philanthropy galas, art collecting

domesticHelp.description: Bi-weekly house cleaning, landscaping, pool service, personal assistant tasks

luxuryExtras.description: Hill Country ranch weekends, quarterly travel, wine cellar curation, philanthropic giving

5M.lifestyle: A large home in Terrell Hills or a renovated King William mansion, a housekeeper several times a week, and a nice car. You dine at the city's best restaurants regularly, hold concierge medical coverage, and fly business class for longer trips. Weekend drives to a Hill Country cabin are easy to budget. San Antonio's low costs and zero state income tax mean this level of spending provides a very comfortable, well-staffed life with significant room for travel and giving.

housing.description: Historic estate in Alamo Heights or a sprawling Hill Country ranch compound minutes from the city

dining.description: Full-time personal chef, catered events, private dining experiences, exclusive culinary travel

healthcare.description: Top-tier concierge medical practice, executive wellness programs, holistic health specialists

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, ranch vehicles, private car service, chartered flights from SAT

entertainment.description: Private suite at the AT&T Center, major philanthropy, art collecting, international travel, exclusive events

domesticHelp.description: Full-time estate manager, housekeeper, personal assistant, ranch hand, grounds and pool crew

luxuryExtras.description: Hill Country ranch operation, first-class international travel, art patronage, angel investing, major philanthropy

10M.lifestyle: A large estate in Alamo Heights, a cook who comes in several times a week, regular domestic help, and a premium healthcare plan with concierge access. You dine at the best restaurants in the city without checking prices, travel first class, and keep a nice car or two. In San Antonio, this budget goes very far -- you live as a successful professional who never thinks about daily costs, with plenty left over for charitable giving and regular travel. Texas's zero income tax keeps every withdrawal dollar intact.
